jaipur.title: Historical Jaipur
activities: Amber Fort: Start your day by visiting the magnificent Amber Fort, located on a hilltop about 11 kilometers from the city center.
Jaigarh Fort: After exploring the Amber Fort, head to Jaigarh Fort, which is nearby. This fort offers stunning panoramic views.
Jal Mahal: On your way back to the city, make a quick stop at Jal Mahal, the Water Palace.
City Palace: Visit the City Palace, the residence of the Maharaja of Jaipur. The palace complex showcases a beautiful blend of Rajasthani and Mughal architecture.

jaipur.title: Cultural Jaipur
activities: Hawa Mahal: Start your day at the Hawa Mahal, also known as the Palace of Winds.
Albert Hall Museum: Visit the Albert Hall Museum, one of the oldest museums in Rajasthan.
Birla Mandir: Head to the Birla Mandir, a beautiful white-marble temple dedicated to Lord Vishnu and Goddess Lakshmi.
Chokhi Dhani: Experience the rich Rajasthani culture and traditions at Chokhi Dhani, an ethnic village resort.

jaipur.title: Shopping and Farewell
activities: Jaipur Bazaars: Explore the vibrant bazaars of Jaipur for some shopping (Johari Bazaar, Bapu Bazaar, Nehru Bazaar).
Nahargarh Fort: In the afternoon, visit Nahargarh Fort, which offers panoramic views of the city.
Sunset at Panna Meena Ka Kund: Witness a beautiful sunset at Panna Meena Ka Kund, an ancient stepwell.
Light and Sound Show at Amer Fort: End your trip with a mesmerizing light and sound show at Amer Fort.

kerala.title: Arrival in Kochi
activities: Arrive at Kochi International Airport.
Meet and greet by our representative, who will assist with the transfer to your hotel.
Relax and unwind after the journey.
Optional: Explore the local markets and nearby attractions.

kerala.title: Kochi Sightseeing
activities: Visit the Mattancherry Palace, also known as the Dutch Palace.
Explore the historic Jewish Synagogue in the Jew Town.
Walk through the charming streets of Fort Kochi, lined with colonial-era buildings.
Witness the iconic Chinese Fishing Nets in action.
Enjoy a Kathakali dance performance in the evening (optional).

kerala.title: Munnar
activities: Proceed to Munnar, a picturesque hill station known for its tea plantations and scenic beauty.
En route, visit the Cheeyappara and Valara waterfalls.
Check-in at the hotel and relax for the day.
Enjoy the cool weather and serene surroundings of Munnar.

kerala.title: Munnar Sightseeing
activities: Explore the breathtaking beauty of Munnar.
Visit the Tata Tea Museum to learn about the history of tea cultivation in the region.
Enjoy a visit to the Echo Point, where your voice echoes back to you.
Visit the Mattupetty Dam and the Kundala Lake.
Optional: Take a scenic ride through the tea plantations on a shikara boat.

kerala.title: Thekkady (Periyar)
activities: Proceed to Thekkady, known for the Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary.
Check-in at the hotel and relax for a while.
In the afternoon, go for a thrilling boating experience on Periyar Lake, where you might spot various wildlife species.

kerala.title: Alleppey Backwaters
activities: Drive to Alleppey, also known as Alappuzha, famous for its backwaters and houseboat cruises.
Embark on an enchanting overnight houseboat cruise through the backwaters.
Enjoy the serene surroundings, lush green landscapes, and delicious Kerala cuisine prepared on the houseboat.

kerala.title: Departure
activities: Disembark from the houseboat after breakfast.
Transfer to Kochi International Airport for your onward journey with beautiful memories of your Kerala trip.

ladakh.title: Arrival in Leh
activities: Arrive at Kushok Bakula Rimpochee Airport in Leh, the capital of Ladakh.
Transfer to your hotel and take some rest to acclimatize to the high altitude.
Explore the local market and Leh Palace in the evening.
Overnight stay in Leh.

ladakh.title: Leh Local Sightseeing
activities: Visit Shanti Stupa for stunning panoramic views of Leh and surrounding landscapes.
Explore the ancient Shey Palace and Hemis Monastery, two important landmarks in the region.
Visit Thiksey Monastery, known for its impressive architecture and spiritual significance.
Return to Leh and rest for the night.

ladakh.title: Leh to Nubra Valley via Khardung La
activities: Embark on a scenic drive to Nubra Valley.
Cross the famous Khardung La, one of the world's highest motorable passes.
Reach Nubra Valley, known for its unique landscape and sand dunes.
Visit Diskit Monastery and enjoy a camel safari in the sand dunes of Hunder.
Overnight stay in a hotel/camp in Nubra Valley.

ladakh.title: Nubra Valley to Pangong Lake
activities: Drive towards Pangong Lake, one of the most breathtaking lakes in Ladakh.
The route takes you through picturesque villages and mesmerizing landscapes.
Arrive at Pangong Lake and spend some time admiring the changing colors of the lake.
Overnight stay in a camp near Pangong Lake.

ladakh.title: Pangong Lake to Leh
activities: Wake up early to witness the beautiful sunrise over Pangong Lake.
After breakfast, drive back to Leh.
On the way, you can visit the famous Hemis Monastery and the school featured in the movie '3 Idiots'.
Reach Leh and spend the rest of the day at leisure.
Overnight stay in Leh.

ladakh.title: Departure
activities: After breakfast, check out from the hotel and transfer to the airport for your onward journey.

meghalaya.title: Arrival in Guwahati - Transfer to Shillong
activities: Arrive at Guwahati Airport/Railway Station.
Meet and greet by the tour representative.
Transfer to Shillong, the capital city of Meghalaya (approximately 3 hours drive).
Check-in at the hotel, relax and freshen up.
Explore the local markets and enjoy the pleasant weather of Shillong.
Overnight stay in Shillong.

meghalaya.title: Shillong Sightseeing
activities: Proceed for a full-day sightseeing tour of Shillong.
Visit Shillong Peak for a panoramic view of the city.
Explore the Don Bosco Museum, showcasing the culture and heritage of the region.
Visit Ward's Lake for a peaceful walk and boating (optional).
Explore the beautiful Elephant Falls.
Return to the hotel and overnight stay in Shillong.

meghalaya.title: Cherrapunjee Excursion
activities: Take a day trip to Cherrapunjee, one of the wettest places on Earth.
Visit the Mawsmai Caves, known for their unique limestone formations.
Explore the Seven Sisters Falls, Nohkalikai Falls (the tallest plunge waterfall in India), and Dainthlen Falls.
Visit the Living Root Bridge of Mawsynram or Cherrapunjee.
Return to Shillong in the evening and overnight stay.

meghalaya.title: Mawlynnong Village and Dawki Excursion
activities: Proceed to Mawlynnong Village, renowned as 'Asia's Cleanest Village'.
Explore the picturesque village and marvel at the living root bridge.
Visit the nearby balancing rock and enjoy breathtaking views from the Skywalk viewpoint.
Later, head to Dawki, known for its crystal-clear Umngot River.
Enjoy a boat ride on the Umngot River (optional) and witness the surreal beauty of the riverbed.
Return to Shillong and overnight stay.

meghalaya.title: Departure
activities: After breakfast, check out from the hotel.
Transfer back to Guwahati Airport/Railway Station.
Bid farewell to Meghalaya with beautiful memories of the trip.

rann-of-kutch.title: Arrival in Bhuj
activities: Arrive at Bhuj airport or railway station.
Transfer to your pre-booked hotel/resort in Bhuj and check-in.
Rest and freshen up.
In the evening, visit the Bhujodi village, known for its traditional handicrafts like weaving and embroidery.
Explore the local market for shopping traditional textiles and handicrafts.
Overnight stay in Bhuj.

rann-of-kutch.title: Rann of Kutch Excursion
activities: After breakfast, depart for a full-day excursion to the Great Rann of Kutch, the world's largest salt desert.
Visit the White Rann, and if traveling during the Rann Utsav, enjoy cultural performances and activities.
Take a camel ride on the vast expanse of the salt desert and witness the breathtaking sunset.
Return to Bhuj in the evening.
Overnight stay in Bhuj.

rann-of-kutch.title: Bhuj Sightseeing and Departure
activities: After breakfast, check out from the hotel and proceed for local sightseeing in Bhuj.
Visit the Aina Mahal, a stunning palace with intricate mirror work and beautiful architecture.
Explore the Prag Mahal, a grand palace showcasing a blend of Italian and Indian architectural styles.
Visit the Kutch Museum, which houses a diverse collection of artifacts, textiles, and crafts from the region.
If time permits, visit the Bhuj Chattardi, a collection of cenotaphs dedicated to the royal family of Kutch.
In the evening, transfer to the Bhuj airport/railway station for departure.
